concept.title: Core concepts
concept.shortdesc: Understand the main resources used by the Data Sync Service API.

section.title: Connector
section.p: A connector represents credentials and configuration for a system you want to read from (source) or write to (target).
Examples: PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, S3, REST.

section.title: Job
section.p: A job defines what to sync: source, target, dataset/table mappings, incremental strategy, and schedule (optional).
Jobs are configuration objects and do not move data until you create a run.

section.title: Run
section.p: A run represents a single execution attempt of a job. Runs are asynchronous and have a lifecycle:
Runs include metrics such as start/end timestamps, rows read/written, and error details (if any).

section.title: Backfill
section.p: A backfill run syncs historical data for a time range (for example, the last 30 days) rather than only the latest increment.

section.title: Replay
section.p: A replay run re-executes a previous run attempt, typically after fixing a transient issue (for example, credentials rotation).
